title: executor: for global index update partition keys but returns "Duplicate Key" error
description: ## Bug Report Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks! ### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required) ```SQL drop table if exists test_global; create table test_global ( a int, b int, c int) partition by range( a ) ( partition p1 values less than (10), partition p2 values less than (20), partition p3 values less than (30) ); alter table test_global add unique index idx_b (b); insert into test_global values (1, 1, 1), (8, 8, 8), (11, 11, 11), (12, 12, 12); update test_global set a = 2 where a = 11; ``` ### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required) update successfully. ### 3. What did you see instead (Required) Duplicate Key error. ### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
poc: create table test_global ( a int, b int, c int)
alter table test_global add unique index idx_b (b);
update test_global set a = 2 where a = 11;
key_bug_features: Updating partitioned table triggers Duplicate Key error
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_partitioned (id INT, name VARCHAR(50), value DECIMAL(10,2)) PARTITION BY RANGE (id) (PARTITION p0 VALUES LESS THAN (10), PARTITION p1 VALUES LESS THAN (20), PARTITION p2 VALUES LESS THAN (30));
ALTER TABLE test_partitioned ADD UNIQUE INDEX idx_name (name);
UPDATE test_partitioned SET name = 'B' WHERE id = 15;
